skill.name: harden
skill.description: Improve interface resilience through better error handling, i18n support, text overflow handling, and edge case management. Makes interfaces robust and production-ready.
skill.summary: Harden user interfaces against real production edge cases.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found a Markdown-only frontend hardening guide with CSS, JSX, JavaScript, and HTML examples, not executable scripts. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences or inline formatting for CSS, JSX, JavaScript, and HTML examples. I found no Ruby shell backticks, command invocation, or user-controlled shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is visible in Markdown examples and prose only. There is no executable Ruby, shell command, subprocess call, or instruction to run external commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardening Language Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged locations discuss interface resilience, long text, ARIA, and global design. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, insecure random generation, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is user interface guidance, not cryptographic code. No weak algorithm name or cryptographic API appears at the cited lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: UI Layout Guidance Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are about flex, grid, typography, and text container sizing. I found no host discovery, OS probing, file enumeration, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is plain frontend design guidance. It does not request system commands, environment inspection, or collection of machine details.

content.value_statement: Many interfaces fail when data is long, missing, translated, slow, or error-prone. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical UI hardening checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews interfaces for long text, empty states, large datasets, and concurrent actions.
Guides improvements for error states, retries, validation, and permission failures.
Checks internationalization risks, including RTL layout, CJK text, and locale formats.
Suggests accessibility resilience for keyboard use, screen readers, motion, and contrast.
Provides testing strategies for slow networks, browser support, and visual regressions.
limitations: Does not run automated tests or browser checks by itself.
Does not provide a complete design system or component library.
Requires project context to make precise implementation changes.
Does not replace security review for server-side validation or authorization.

use_cases.title: Prepare a feature for launch
use_cases.description: Review a new interface for missing states, error recovery, text overflow, and slow network behavior before release.

use_cases.title: Improve global product readiness
use_cases.description: Find layout and formatting risks that appear with translations, RTL languages, CJK text, and locale-specific numbers.

use_cases.title: Audit user flows after defects
use_cases.description: Identify weak states after reports about failed submissions, empty data, duplicate clicks, or unclear errors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic hardening review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one screen or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the harden skill to review this interface for production edge cases. Focus on long text, empty states, loading states, and clear error messages.

prompt_templates.title: Internationalization check
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a UI that must support global users.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the harden skill to check this feature for internationalization issues. Include long translations, RTL layout, CJK characters, date formats, and number formats.

prompt_templates.title: Failure state improvement
prompt_templates.scenario: Strengthen a flow with network and API failures.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the harden skill to improve this flow for offline use, slow requests, API errors, validation failures, permission errors, and retry behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Release readiness audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before shipping a complex interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the harden skill to perform a release readiness audit. Cover accessibility resilience, performance limits, concurrent operations, browser support, and automated test gaps.

output_examples.input: Review a dashboard card list before launch.
output: The list needs empty, loading, and API error states.
Card titles need wrapping rules for long account names.
Bulk actions should prevent duplicate submissions while requests are pending.

output_examples.input: Check a checkout form for resilience.
output: Validation errors should stay near each field and preserve user input.
The submit button should handle slow payment responses and retry safely.
Currency, address, and phone fields need locale-aware formatting.
Fixed-width buttons may fail with longer translations.
Dynamic status messages should be announced to assistive technology.

best_practices: Test with extreme data before polishing visual details.
Design every user action with loading, success, failure, and retry states.
Use semantic HTML, locale-aware formatting, and flexible layout rules.
anti_patterns: Assuming English text length and fixed button widths.
Showing generic errors without recovery actions.
Trusting client-side validation as the only protection.

faq.question: What does this skill help harden?
faq.answer: It helps harden frontend interfaces, flows, forms, lists, dashboards, and user-facing states.

faq.question: Does it change code automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and can support code edits when used inside an agent that has project access.

faq.question: Is this only for accessibility?
faq.answer: No. It also covers errors, internationalization, performance limits, empty states, and concurrent actions.

faq.question: Can it replace QA testing?
faq.answer: No. It improves review coverage, but teams should still run manual and automated tests.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it contain executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is a Markdown guide with examples and instructions.
